name: Intend Indiana, INC
description_short: Nonprofit CDFI connecting underserved Hoosiers to affordable housing, small business lending, and community development through equitable financing and technical assistance.
description_long:
Intend Indiana is a nonprofit organization formed in 2020 through the merger of Renew Indianapolis and King Park Development Corporation. The organization operates as a community development financial institution (CDFI) certified by the U.S. Department of the Treasury, positioning it to serve populations traditionally underserved by conventional lending markets. Based in Indianapolis at 1704 Bellefontaine Street, Intend Indiana has established itself as a mission-driven lender and developer focused on equitable community development.

The organization operates four distinct programs: Affordable HomeMatters provides equitable homeownership opportunities and housing development support; Build Fund offers small business lending and technical assistance to underserved entrepreneurs; Edge Fund provides flexible affordable housing financing solutions; and Renew Landbank helps communities address vacant and abandoned properties through land bank management, technical assistance, and policy advising. Each program is designed to address specific community development gaps while reinforcing Intend's core mission of connecting people to opportunity.

Intend Indiana distinguishes itself through its integrated approach combining financing with technical assistance and community partnerships. As a certified CDFI, Build Fund and Edge Fund can offer more flexible terms than traditional lenders, and the organization reinvests loan repayments back into communities to create sustainable economic cycles. The organization maintains transparency through published audits (2016-2024) and IRS Form 990s, demonstrates accountability through documented core values emphasizing collaboration and equity, and operates as a chartered member of NeighborWorks America, a national network of community development organizations.

Intend Indiana is fundamentally a nonprofit lender and community development organization rather than a consumer finance company offering quick cash or credit repair. Those seeking conventional personal loans, emergency cash advances, or credit repair services will not find these products here. The organization's strength lies in serving entrepreneurs, prospective homebuyers, and community revitalization efforts through patient capital and technical support rather than rapid, transaction-based lending.
pros: Certified CDFI status enables more flexible lending terms than traditional banks for underserved borrowers
Integrated approach combines financing with technical assistance and expert guidance
Reinvests loan repayments back into communities, creating sustainable economic growth cycles
Four distinct programs address housing development, small business, affordable housing financing, and land redevelopment
Published annual audits (2016-2024) and IRS Form 990s demonstrate financial transparency and accountability
Chartered member of NeighborWorks America, a nationally recognized community development network
Explicit anti-discrimination policy with federal compliance under Treasury Department oversight
cons: Not designed for emergency cash needs or rapid same-day funding like payday lenders or emergency cash services
Limited to specific programs (housing, small business, affordable housing financing, land banks) rather than universal consumer lending
No credit repair or credit-building products for consumers looking to improve existing credit scores
Requires engagement with community development mission; not suitable for conventional personal loan borrowing
Website provides limited information about specific loan terms, rates, or eligibility requirements for prospective borrowers
best_for: Small business owners in Indiana seeking equitable financing with technical assistance rather than conventional bank loans
First-time homebuyers in underserved communities looking for affordable housing solutions and homeownership education
Community organizations and local governments addressing vacant property revitalization and land bank development
Nonprofits and developers focused on affordable housing creation and neighborhood stabilization
diagnosis: Intend Indiana is best for entrepreneurs, prospective homebuyers, and community organizations in Indiana seeking mission-driven lending with technical support rather than conventional consumer finance products. The primary caveat is that this is a specialized nonprofit CDFI focused on community development—not a consumer lending platform for personal loans, credit repair, or emergency cash, and loan availability is limited to specific qualifying programs and community development purposes.
services: Small business lending through Build Fund (certified CDFI)
Technical assistance and expert guidance for small businesses
Affordable housing financing through Edge Fund (certified CDFI)
Homeownership education and support through Affordable HomeMatters
Equitable affordable housing development and solutions
Land bank management services through Renew Landbank
Technical assistance for vacant and abandoned property redevelopment
Policy advising for community land bank establishment
Community capacity building and partnership coordination
Flexible financing for housing preservation and creation
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
